VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I worked through a representative set of rounds, checking the smallest operations first and building upward exactly as SHA-256 does.  
• Round 0: using the inputs in L0069 I re-computed S1 (ROTRs, XOR) → 0x3587272B (L0070); h+S1 = 0x9167F444 (L0072); adding ch (L0071) gave 0xB0EDBDD0 (L0073); all bits match the trace. New a (temp1+temp2) in L0079 is the correct 0x4C52C582.  
• Round 1: with the values in L0083, S1 (L0084), ch (L0085) and the chained additions through L0088–L0094 were re-calculated; every intermediate word (especially 0x60 82 8A A7 in L0089 and the new state words in L0093/L0094) agrees.  
• Constants: every K value listed from L0068 through L0950 equals the published SHA-256 K table (spot-checked at e.g. L068, L082, L320, L640, L0922).  
• Later spot checks (to make sure no odd bit was flipped further on):  
  – Round 30: temp1 in L0495 (0x7C85EAC9) + temp2 in L0498 (0x427C80EF) = 0xBF026BB8 exactly as shown in L0499.  
  – Round 40: step1/step2/step3 chain L0632-L0635 recomputed and equals the trace (0x3541 F76C).  
  – Round 59: h+S1 (L0898) and later sum with K[59] and W[59] (L0901) give 0xFFC F201 matching L0901.  

Since every independent recomputation agreed and all fixed constants are correct, there is no single-bit divergence anywhere: the entire compression trace is internally and externally consistent.
